title: Creamy Pesto Chicken with Roasted Tomatoes (High-Protein Recipe) | Eati
introduction: Creamy Pesto Chicken with Roasted Tomatoes combines tender chicken with pesto, roasted tomatoes, and a creamy sauce feel. It is filling, high-protein, and made to be meal-prep friendly.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- Chicken
- Pesto
- Roasted tomatoes
- Garlic
- Parmesan
- Herbs
- Creamy sauce base
- Salt and pepper

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Season chicken and cook until browned and cooked through.
2. Add garlic and herbs; stir to develop aroma.
3. Warm pesto and mix into the sauce base.
4. Stir in roasted tomatoes and parmesan.
5. Combine with chicken, simmer briefly, then serve and log in Eati.

sections.heading: Calories, Fat, and Protein (Estimated)
sections.content:
Calories: 642 kcal per serving (estimated)
Fat: 43g
Protein: 50g

Tip: parmesan and cream drive fat calories; portion carefully.

sections.heading: Understanding the Macros: Fats Are Doing the Heavy Lifting
sections.content:
This recipe has a notably high fat content (43g per serving) driven by three ingredients: pesto, parmesan, and the creamy sauce base. Understanding where those calories come from lets you decide how to tweak the recipe for your goals.

Fat breakdown per serving (roughly):
• Pesto (2 tbsp): ~18g fat, 160 cal — primarily olive oil and pine nuts.
• Parmesan (2 tbsp grated): ~4g fat, 50 cal — saturated, but flavor-dense.
• Heavy cream or cream-based sauce (1/4 cup): ~22g fat, 200 cal — most concentrated calorie source.
• Chicken breast: ~3g fat, naturally lean.

When this macro profile works:
• Maintenance or small deficits (TDEE > 2,200 cal)
• Low-carb or keto approaches (high fat is the point)
• People doing heavy resistance training who need calorie density

When to lighten it:
• Aggressive fat loss (TDEE < 2,000 cal)
• Days after a heavy restaurant meal
• When paired with pasta or another carb-heavy side

Use a TDEE calculator to see whether this calorie profile fits your week. For protein-forward lighter alternatives, see [high-protein low-calorie foods](/blog/high-protein-low-calorie-foods).

sections.heading: Three Lighter Versions of Creamy Pesto Chicken
sections.content:
You can transform this recipe into different calorie ranges:

Lighter Version 1 — 'Sub the cream' (–180 cal per serving):
• Replace heavy cream with 1/4 cup light cream cheese blended with 1/4 cup milk.
• Still creamy, cuts fat nearly in half.
• New macros: ~460 cal, 52g protein, 25g fat per serving.

Lighter Version 2 — 'Greek yogurt swap' (–220 cal per serving):
• Replace heavy cream with 1/3 cup non-fat Greek yogurt added off-heat.
• Boosts protein another 6–8g.
• New macros: ~420 cal, 58g protein, 18g fat per serving.

Lighter Version 3 — 'Pesto lite' (–100 cal per serving):
• Cut pesto to 1 tbsp per serving (instead of 2).
• Add extra fresh basil + lemon juice to preserve flavor.
• New macros: ~542 cal, 50g protein, 32g fat per serving.

Maximum reduction (combining all three techniques): ~350 cal, 58g protein, 15g fat per serving. That's the same protein at roughly half the calories.

sections.heading: What to Serve With Creamy Pesto Chicken
sections.content:
Given the richness of this dish, pair it with lower-calorie, high-volume sides:

Lower-calorie pairings (50–150 cal):
• Arugula salad with lemon vinaigrette
• Roasted or steamed broccoli
• Spiralized zucchini noodles (raw or lightly sautéed)
• Roasted cherry tomatoes and spinach
• Grilled asparagus with a squeeze of lemon

Moderate-calorie carb pairings (200–300 cal):
• Cauliflower gnocchi (~200 cal per serving)
• Orzo tossed with spinach (1/2 cup cooked, ~100 cal)
• Whole-wheat pappardelle or fettuccine (1/2 cup cooked, ~100 cal)
• Quinoa with fresh herbs
• Polenta (1/3 cup, ~100 cal)

Balanced plate for a heavy day: 1 serving creamy pesto chicken + 2 cups arugula + 1/4 cup shaved parmesan salad = ~700 cal with 56g protein. Filling enough to skip dessert.

Lower-calorie plate: Use Lighter Version 2 + spiralized zucchini + fresh basil = ~480 cal, 62g protein. See [best foods for weight loss](/blog/best-foods-for-weight-loss) for more low-calorie sides.

sections.heading: How This Differs from Marry Me Chicken & Tuscan Chicken
sections.content:
Three popular creamy Italian-style chicken recipes — how do they compare?

Creamy Pesto Chicken (this recipe):
• 642 cal, 50g protein, 43g fat
• Flavor profile: basil-forward, herbaceous, bright
• Signature ingredient: pesto

[Crockpot Marry Me Chicken](/blog/crockpot-marry-me-chicken-recipe):
• 585 cal, 48g protein, 38g fat
• Flavor profile: rich, sun-dried tomato, garlicky
• Signature ingredients: sun-dried tomatoes + parmesan

[Crockpot Tuscan Chicken](/blog/crockpot-tuscan-chicken-recipe):
• 446 cal, 44g protein, 28g fat
• Flavor profile: balanced Italian, spinach-forward
• Signature ingredients: sun-dried tomato + spinach

Pick based on:
• Lowest calories → Tuscan Chicken
• Most herbaceous flavor → Creamy Pesto Chicken
• Richest/most decadent → Marry Me Chicken
• Best for meal prep → Tuscan or Marry Me (slow cooker)
• Best weeknight speed → Creamy Pesto (stovetop)

All three work in rotation — try each once and pick your favorite for weekly rotation. For more high-protein weeknight ideas, see [30 best high-protein recipes](/blog/30-best-high-protein-recipes).

sections.heading: Meal Prep Tips
sections.content:
Creamy pesto chicken holds up reasonably well in the fridge, though texture is best on day 1–2.

Batch cooking:
• Double the recipe for 8 servings. The same sauce ratio works.
• Cook chicken in batches — don't crowd the pan, which prevents browning.
• Finish sauce separately and combine at the end.

Storage:
• Fridge: 3–4 days.
• Freezer: Up to 2 months. Note: cream-based sauces can separate slightly on thawing — stir well when reheating.

Reheating:
• Stovetop (best): Heat on medium-low with 2–3 tbsp water or milk to loosen the sauce. Stir continuously.
• Microwave: 90 seconds on 70% power, stir, 30 more seconds. High power can split the sauce.

Double-duty tips:
• Toss leftovers with pasta for a quick lunch.
• Stuff into a whole-wheat pita with arugula.
• Chop and add to a grain bowl with rice and vegetables.

Log portion sizes accurately in Eati — the variability in pasta or bread pairings can swing total calories by 300+. Read [how to track calories correctly](/blog/how-to-track-calories-correctly) for best practices.

conclusion: This dish is big on flavor and protein, making it a strong choice for building consistent macros. Track it in Eati.
metaTitle: Creamy Pesto Chicken with Roasted Tomatoes: 50g Protein (642 Cal, 3 Lighter Versions)
metaDescription: Creamy pesto chicken with roasted tomatoes — 50g protein per serving. Includes 3 lighter versions (down to 350 cal), side pairing guide, and comparison with Tuscan and Marry Me Chicken.
targetKeyword: creamy pesto chicken roasted tomatoes high protein

faqs.question: How many calories in creamy pesto chicken with roasted tomatoes?
faqs.answer: This recipe: about 642 calories per serving with 50g protein and 43g fat (using standard pesto, heavy cream, and parmesan). Using Greek yogurt + 1 tbsp pesto instead cuts this to ~420 calories while adding protein.

faqs.question: Can I make pesto chicken without cream?
faqs.answer: Yes. Options: non-fat Greek yogurt (highest protein, added off heat), light cream cheese blended with milk, or a mix of chicken broth and parmesan (lightest). Each gives a different texture but all deliver creaminess without heavy cream's 400+ cal per cup.

faqs.question: Is pesto healthy for weight loss?
faqs.answer: Pesto is calorie-dense (80 cal per tablespoon) but nutrient-rich — olive oil, basil, garlic, pine nuts, parmesan. In moderate portions (1–2 tbsp per serving), it fits any fat-loss plan and adds heart-healthy monounsaturated fats. The issue is using too much, not using it at all.

faqs.question: What goes with creamy pesto chicken?
faqs.answer: For lower calories: spiralized zucchini, arugula salad, roasted broccoli, or cauliflower gnocchi. For heartier meals: 1/2 cup whole-wheat pasta, orzo, quinoa, or polenta. Pair with lighter sides if you're keeping the full-fat recipe, carby sides if you're using the lightened version.

faqs.question: Can I use rotisserie chicken for creamy pesto chicken?
faqs.answer: Absolutely — it saves 15+ minutes of cooking time. Shred or chop the rotisserie chicken and add to the warm sauce at the end. Rotisserie chicken is slightly saltier, so reduce added salt. Calories stay very close to the breast-based version.
